pr.title: Migrate `stage` data and most of `ground` data to C
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Migrated all remaining `asm/melee/gr/stage.s` data and much of `asm/melee/gr/ground.s` data into C, removed the stage assembly object from the build, expanded `ground.h` prototypes, added shared ground inlines, and made small include/formatting/tooling adjustments needed for matching. The PR reports `src/melee/gr/stage.c` at 100% and `src/melee/gr/ground.c` at 68.91%, with many ground functions/data now represented in C while some large functions remained as `ASM`/`NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/gr/stage.s` and removed `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/stage.s.o` from `obj_files.mk`, leaving `src/melee/gr/stage.c.o` as the stage object.
Moved the large `unk_arr_803E9960` stage-id table into `src/melee/gr/stage.c` as `struct StructStageIDWithUnks unk_arr_803E9960[]`, replacing the previous external assembly data.

Moved several ground data objects from `asm/melee/gr/ground.s` into `src/melee/gr/ground.c`, including `Ground_803E065C`, `Ground_803E066C`, `Ground_803E0680`, `Ground_803E069C`, `Ground_803E06AC`, `Ground_804D4500`, `Ground_804D4508`, `Ground_804D450C`, `Ground_804D4514`, `Ground_804D451C`, `Ground_804D4524`, and `Ground_804D452C`.

Kept only a small remaining `Ground_803E06C8` block in `asm/melee/gr/ground.s`, updating its references from local labels like `.L_804D451C`/`.L_804D450C` to C symbols `Ground_804D451C`/`Ground_804D450C`.
Expanded `src/melee/gr/ground.h` with many public `Ground_801Cxxxx` prototypes and `extern StageInfo stage_info`, making cross-file references explicit.

Added `src/melee/gr/inlines.h` with `GET_GROUND(gobj)` and `Ground_JObjInline1`, then included it in several target-test ground files (`grtcaptain`, `grtdonkey`, `grtemblem`, `grtfalco`, `grticeclimber`, `grtluigi`, `grtmewtwo`, `grtpeach`, `grtpichu`) and `ground.c`.
Moved `mpColl_804D64A0`, `mpColl_804D64A4`, `mpColl_804D64A8`, and `mpColl_804D64AC` extern declarations from `mpcoll.c` into `mpcoll.h` and included `mp/mpcoll.h` from `ground.c`.
Adjusted `tools/convert_data.py` defaults for this migration: `input_glob` changed to `**/ground.s`, zero dwords defaulted to `NULL` via `zero: None`, and `find_f64` was disabled.
Made small syntax/spacing tweaks in unrelated files (`MSL/string.c`, `CARDBios.c`, `ftNs_SpecialHi.c`, `ftaction.c`, `lbcollision.c`, `cobj.c`) that appear to be matching-oriented formatting changes.
smart_moves: Converted raw assembly tables into typed C initializers instead of leaving opaque `.4byte` sequences, making `unk_arr_803E9960` and ground data searchable and easier to type-refine later.

Used `SDATA` for C definitions that replaced small-data assembly labels (`Ground_804D450C`, `Ground_804D4514`, `Ground_804D451C`, `Ground_804D4524`, `Ground_804D452C`), preserving placement-sensitive small-data behavior.
Introduced local temporary structs (`struct Node`, `struct a`) around migrated ground data rather than forcing premature final names or full reverse-engineered types.
Centralized repeated ground object access/animation setup in `gr/inlines.h`, reducing duplicate inline bodies across target stage files.
Moved `mpColl_*` globals into the header once `ground.c` needed them, avoiding scattered file-local externs.
Left hard unmatched functions as `ASM` guarded by `MUST_MATCH`/`WIP` instead of pretending they were decompiled, while still allowing data migration and build cleanup to land.
decomp_lessons: Large data-only assembly files can often be eliminated before every function in the adjacent C file is fully matched; migrate the data tables first and keep remaining code as guarded `ASM` if necessary.
When migrating `.sdata` symbols, preserve section intent explicitly with `SDATA` in C or references from remaining assembly may still compile but not match/link as expected.
For raw data conversion, verify how zero dwords should be represented. This PR changed converter behavior from float zero to `NULL`, which better matched pointer-heavy ground data.
Avoid overtyping unknown data on first migration. Placeholder structs with address-based symbol names are acceptable when they preserve layout and unblock further decompilation.
Shared inline helpers should live in a small `inlines.h` with the exact dependencies they require, especially for patterns used across many target/test stage files.
Even tiny whitespace changes (`t& w`, `*0.01745329252F`, label spacing) can matter in this codebase when preserving compiler output.
naming_conventions: Address-based names such as `Ground_803E065C`, `Ground_804D450C`, `Stage_8022519C`, and `Ground_801C4FAC` were retained for symbols without stronger semantic names.
Extern/prototype comments use address prefixes, e.g. `/* 1C4FAC */ void Ground_801C4FAC(void);` and `/* 49E6C8 */ extern StageInfo stage_info;`.
New helper macro name `GET_GROUND(gobj)` parallels existing object-access macros like `GET_JOBJ(gobj)`.
Shared inline helper `Ground_JObjInline1` uses a provisional descriptive-plus-number name, signaling behavior is known enough to share but not semantically finalized.
The stage table kept the historical name `unk_arr_803E9960`, indicating the table’s purpose was still not fully named.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `.4byte` assembly data with equivalent C arrays/structs while preserving symbol names referenced by remaining assembly.
Changed remaining assembly references from local labels to exported C symbols (`Ground_804D451C`, `Ground_804D450C`) after moving the targets into C.
Used exact C literals for migrated floats and ints such as `{ 0, 0.57, 0.57, 0.57, 0 }`, `float Ground_804D4508 = 16`, `0xD0000`, `0x40000`, and `-1`.
Used `SDATA` to force small-data placement for migrated `Ground_804D45xx` labels.
Kept unmatched functions under `ASM` with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` fallback bodies containing `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`.
Added fake assertion string padding in `Stage_80224E64` with a note that it should become `HSD_ASSERT` later, showing a pragmatic matching workaround.
Tuned `convert_data.py` for `ground.s` conversion by treating zeros as `NULL` and disabling f64 detection to avoid misclassifying pointer/int-heavy data.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type `unk_arr_803E9960` / `StructStageIDWithUnks`.
Find later PRs that finish matching `Ground_801C4FAC`, `Ground_801C14D0`, or other 0% ground functions listed in PR #1035.
Search for follow-up cleanup replacing the fake padded assert string in `Stage_80224E64` with `HSD_ASSERT`.
Check whether `struct Node` and `struct a` in `ground.c` were later given semantic names or moved to headers.
Look for later changes to `gr/inlines.h` and uses of `Ground_JObjInline1` across target-test ground files.
